description_long: This object consists of two whole watermelons stacked vertically, with the smaller one resting on top of the larger. Both watermelons have a smooth, glossy rind with alternating dark green and light green stripes running from pole to pole. The surface is unbroken, with no visible cuts, blemishes, or stem attachments. The overall shape is round to slightly oblong, typical of cultivated watermelons. The striping pattern is regular and evenly distributed across both fruits, giving them a fresh and ripe appearance. No interior or seeds are visible, and the watermelons appear intact and undamaged.
description: A pair of whole, stacked watermelons with smooth, glossy green rinds and regular dark and light green striping.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the circular tops of both watermelons and the radiating striped pattern.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the stacked arrangement and the horizontal striping across both watermelons.
description_view_2: Another top-down view, nearly identical to view 0, highlighting the symmetry and striping.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, showing the relative sizes and the clear, uninterrupted rind pattern.
attribute_pattern: Regular, alternating dark and light green striping running lengthwise across the surface.
attribute_rind_markings: No notable blemishes, spots, or unique markings; the rind is clean with only the striped pattern visible.
